sourceMovie.title: SPL: Kill Zone
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit SPL: Kill Zone for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Wilson Yip keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Layer Cake
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If SPL: Kill Zone clicked for you, Layer Cake (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after SPL: Kill Zone?
faq.answer: Try Layer Cake, The Getaway, Master Z: Ip Man Legacy—each shares DNA with SPL: Kill Zone while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is SPL: Kill Zone?
faq.answer: SPL: Kill Zone (2005) is best described as Action, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is SPL: Kill Zone part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "SPL Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
